does: This paper introduces Gemini 3.5, a frontier-class large language model designed to support complex agentic workflows and execution tasks. The model extends Gemini's capabilities to enable agents to perform multi-step reasoning and coordinate tool use across diverse domains. Gemini 3.5 demonstrates improvements in instruction following, planning, and the ability to interact with external systems and APIs. The system is optimized for scenarios where models must break down complex goals into executable sub-tasks, maintain context across extended interactions, and dynamically adapt their approach based on external feedback or changing conditions.
connects: This work builds on prior research in agent-based AI systems and tool-augmented language models, extending lines of work on ReAct prompting, chain-of-thought reasoning, and function calling interfaces. It responds to growing demand for models that go beyond text generation to coordinate real-world actions, building on foundational work in embodied AI and multimodal reasoning. The work also connects to recent advances in scaling language models for complex reasoning and the emerging paradigm of treating LLMs as controllers or planners for broader AI systems, rather than isolated text predictors.
enables: Gemini 3.5 opens pathways toward more autonomous AI systems capable of handling real-world tasks requiring sequential decision-making and tool coordination. The model enables development of autonomous agents for enterprise automation, scientific research coordination, and complex problem-solving workflows. Future work could extend this toward truly long-horizon planning with persistent memory, multi-agent coordination systems, and tighter integration of symbolic reasoning with learned policies. The foundation also supports safety research into agent alignment, monitoring agentic behavior at scale, and verifying correctness of multi-step action sequences.
